attributes.action: Make It Illegal for Employers to Ghost Job Applicants During the UK Jobs Crisis.
attributes.additional_details: UK unemployment is at its highest level in nearly five years, and youth unemployment is around 16%, the highest outside the pandemic in over a decade. Yet many applicants are routinely ghosted after applying or interviewing. People are told to “get into work” while employers face no obligation to respond. This wastes time, increases stress, and undermines trust in the labour market. If the Government is serious about tackling unemployment, it must also enforce basic accountability in recruitment
attributes.background: Introduce legislation requiring employers to acknowledge job applications, provide a decision within a set timeframe, and give basic feedback after interviews. It should not be legal for employers to advertise vacancies, collect applicant data, and then ignore candidates.

rejection.details:
You may wish to sign the following petition, which calls for similar action:

Require employers to send responses to all job applications
https://petition.parliament.uk/petitions/742375
